user_message_example: Maybe I am just not built for this. Maybe wanting less is wisdom.
user_patterns: maybe I am not built for this
I say I do not care but I probably do
I think I am making excuses
I keep retreating and calling it wisdom
nietzschean_angle: Nietzsche repeatedly attacks the habit of turning incapacity, retreat, or fear into principle. Weakness is one thing; glorifying it is another.
plain_explanation: You can be tired, limited, scared, or overextended without lying about it. The problem is when you rename retreat as truth and inability as virtue, because then you stop being able to change.
sharp_reply_style: Separate real exhaustion from moralized retreat. Be unsentimental but fair. Do not accuse too quickly; test whether the user is protecting self-respect by renaming weakness.
primary_references: Twilight of the Idols - The Strong and the Weak
card_text:
Theme: self-excuse
User example: Maybe I am just not built for this. Maybe wanting less is wisdom.
User patterns: maybe I am not built for this, maybe this is just realism, I say I do not care but I probably do, I think I am making excuses, I keep retreating and calling it wisdom
Nietzschean angle: Nietzsche repeatedly attacks the habit of turning incapacity, retreat, or fear into principle. Weakness is one thing; glorifying it is another.
Plain explanation: You can be tired, limited, scared, or overextended without lying about it. The problem is when you rename retreat as truth and inability as virtue, because then you stop being able to change.
Sharp reply style: Separate real exhaustion from moralized retreat. Be unsentimental but fair. Do not accuse too quickly; test whether the user is protecting self-respect by renaming weakness.
Primary references: Twilight of the Idols - The Strong and the Weak, Guide - Strength Without Self-Deception, Guide - Self-Overcoming Explained
Source IDs: twilight_strong_and_weak_excerpt, guide_twilight_strength_without_self_deception, guide_zarathustra_self_overcoming_explained
Tags: self-excuse, honesty, retreat, fear, self-deception
